quiz.question: Default choice for a general-purpose list in Java?
quiz.explanation: ArrayList: O(1) amortized append and O(1) random access. Prefer ArrayDeque for stack/queue.

quiz.question: HashMap average get/put complexity is…
quiz.explanation: Average O(1); worst case degrades with collisions (treeified buckets in Java 8+).

quiz.question: Which structure finds shortest path in an unweighted graph?
quiz.explanation: BFS explores level-by-level and yields shortest path in unweighted graphs.

quiz.question: Best structure for an LRU cache in Java?
quiz.explanation: LinkedHashMap in access-order mode + removeEldestEntry implements LRU cleanly.

quiz.question: PriorityQueue in Java is by default a…
quiz.explanation: Default PriorityQueue is a min-heap; use a custom comparator for max-heap.

quiz.question: Binary search requires the input to be…
quiz.explanation: Binary search halves a sorted search space each step → O(log n).

quiz.question: Two-pointer technique is especially useful for…
options: Problems on sorted arrays / strings with left-right scans
quiz.explanation: Classic for pair sums, palindromes, container-with-most-water, etc.

quiz.question: Sliding window typically improves which complexity?
options: O(n²) nested scans → O(n) for contiguous subarray problems
quiz.explanation: Maintain a window and expand/shrink instead of recomputing from scratch.

quiz.question: Dijkstra’s algorithm needs which structure for efficiency?
quiz.explanation: Always expand the currently closest unvisited node via a min-heap.

quiz.question: Dynamic programming is best described as…
options: Overlapping subproblems + optimal substructure with memo/tabulation
quiz.explanation: DP stores subproblem answers to avoid exponential recomputation.

quiz.question: Best first step on a vague interview problem?
quiz.explanation: Requirements clarity prevents solving the wrong problem.

quiz.question: When stuck, a strong tactic is…
options: Work a tiny example aloud and state invariants

quiz.question: After a brute-force solution you should…
quiz.explanation: Correct → then efficient. Interviewers value the path.

quiz.question: Rubber-duck debugging is…
options: Explaining the problem out loud to find gaps
quiz.explanation: Articulating assumptions often reveals the bug.

quiz.question: Trade-off questions test whether you can…
options: Name costs/benefits and choose for the context
quiz.explanation: Engineering is trade-offs under constraints (latency, cost, complexity).

problems.title: Choose the Right Structure
problems.prompt: You need Top-K most expensive orders from a stream of millions. Which structure and why?
problems.solution: Min-heap (PriorityQueue) of size K: O(n log K). Keep the K largest by evicting the current minimum when a larger value arrives.

problems.title: HashMap Contract
problems.prompt: You use a custom UserKey as HashMap key but only override equals(). What breaks and how do you fix it?
problems.solution: Equal keys may land in different buckets → duplicates / failed lookups. Override both hashCode() and equals() consistently.

problems.title: Complexity Callout
problems.prompt: A nested loop over an array of size n does constant work inside. State time and space complexity.
problems.solution: Time O(n²), space O(1) if no extra structures proportional to n.

problems.title: Anagram Check Approach
problems.prompt: Describe an O(n) approach to check if two strings are anagrams (same letters).
hints: Counting beats sorting for ASCII/limited alphabet.
problems.solution: Count character frequencies in one string (+1) and the other (−1). All counts zero ⇒ anagram. O(n) time, O(k) space.

problems.title: Clarify the Prompt
problems.prompt: Interviewer: “Design a feed.” List 8 clarifying questions you ask before drawing boxes.
problems.solution: DAU? RPS? Media? Ranking? Real-time? Consistency? Personalization? Retention window? Mobile offline? Abuse?

problems.title: Brute → Better
problems.prompt: Find two numbers in an array that sum to target. Give O(n²) then O(n) approaches.
problems.solution: Brute: nested loops. Better: one pass HashMap value→index; check target−x seen. O(n) time, O(n) space.
